codeReview.summary: The commit improves supervisor provenance, but the implementation still relies on process-global MCP state and regresses service-generated work creation provenance. The live MCP path is therefore not fully request-scoped, and the new tests still stop short of exercising the production handler end-to-end.

issues.description: CreatedBy() is driven by a shared internalSessionID on the Server, and serve.go reuses that same server instance for both the supervisor loop and the external /mcp endpoint. That means provenance is still a global switch rather than per-call caller/session context; any concurrent external MCP mutation while the supervisor session is active can be mislabeled ActorSupervisor.

CreateWork now maps req.CreatedBy through actorFromCreatedBy, but several service-generated CreateWork call sites still omit CreatedBy (for example doc auto-create here and attestation child creation at line 6000, plus bootstrap.go:204). Those paths regress from ActorService to ActorWorker, so service follow-on provenance is no longer trustworthy.

The new provenance tests still only assert CreatedBy()/actorFromCreatedBy() helper behavior. They do not invoke the actual MCP handler via session.CallTool or the serve HTTP transport, so the requested live-path end-to-end coverage is still missing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/library/contracts.md still describes the MCP provenance fix as a session-context problem, but the current implementation uses a process-global internalSessionID instead of request-scoped context. The shared note should be updated once the implementation is corrected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: contracts.md Event Provenance section; internal/mcpserver/server.go:80-99; internal/cli/serve.go:281-295

sharedStateObservations.observation: The control-plane-worker procedure calls for provenance verification across CLI, HTTP, MCP, and service-generated follow-on paths, but the added tests remain helper-only. A stronger reminder to exercise live MCP session.CallTool behavior would match this feature class better.

summary: FAIL: the fix does not yet provide trustworthy, request-scoped MCP provenance end-to-end; it also regresses provenance for service-generated work creation paths.
